tasks.description: Initialize backend project: Python virtualenv, FastAPI app skeleton, project structure, SQLAlchemy setup, Alembic config, Docker Compose for PostgreSQL, health check endpoint
acceptance_criteria: FastAPI app starts with uvicorn on port 8000
GET /health returns 200 OK
Docker Compose starts PostgreSQL and backend containers
Alembic can generate and apply migrations
Database connection is confirmed at startup

tasks.description: Initialize frontend project: Vite + React + TypeScript scaffold, Tailwind CSS setup, shadcn/ui init, React Router setup, Zustand store skeleton, project directory structure
acceptance_criteria: Dev server starts with `npm run dev`
Tailwind CSS classes render correctly
shadcn/ui components render (e.g. Button)
React Router navigates between stubbed pages
Docker build succeeds

tasks.description: Implement authentication system: User model, JWT token generation/verification, registration endpoint, login endpoint, current-user endpoint, password hashing, role/permission model (admin, agent, user)
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/auth/register creates a new user
POST /api/v1/auth/login returns JWT access/refresh tokens
GET /api/v1/auth/me returns current user profile
JWT token expiry and refresh work correctly
Password is bcrypt-hashed in database
Role-based access decorator works

tasks.description: Implement frontend authentication: Login page, Register page, auth context/store, protected route wrapper, API client with JWT interceptor, token refresh logic
acceptance_criteria: User can register from the frontend form
User can log in and token is stored
Protected routes redirect to login when unauthenticated
Access token is attached to all API requests
Expired token triggers automatic refresh
Logout clears tokens and redirects to login

tasks.description: Implement base frontend layout: sidebar navigation, top header with user menu, breadcrumbs, responsive layout shell, reusable page wrapper
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar shows navigation links for all modules
Header shows logged-in user name and logout button
Breadcrumbs reflect current route hierarchy
Layout is responsive (collapses on mobile)
Active route is highlighted in sidebar

tasks.description: Implement backend shared infrastructure: common CRUD base service, pagination utility, error handlers, response envelope, permission checking middleware, audit log model
acceptance_criteria: BaseService provides create/read/update/delete/list methods
Pagination returns page/page_size/total/items envelope
Custom error handlers return consistent error JSON
Audit log records create/update/delete operations with user_id and timestamp

tasks.description: Implement Incident Management data models: Incident model, IncidentStatusLog model, Priority matrix model, SLA configuration model, database migrations
acceptance_criteria: Incident model has all fields: id, title, description, status, priority, category, assigned_to, reported_by, resolution, sla_deadline, timestamps
IncidentStatusLog tracks all status changes with timestamp and user
Priority matrix model defines impact × urgency → priority mapping
SLA config stores target resolution times per priority level
Migrations are reversible (upgrade + downgrade)

tasks.description: Implement Incident status workflow engine: state machine with valid transitions (New→Assigned→InProgress→Resolved→Closed), transition validation, automatic status logging, re-open logic, notification triggers
acceptance_criteria: Workflow defines ALL valid status transitions
Invalid transitions raise explicit 400 error with message
Each transition creates an IncidentStatusLog entry
Resolved→Closed requires confirmation or auto-closes after SLA
Re-open from Closed→InProgress is allowed with reason

tasks.description: Implement Incident priority matrix and SLA timer: priority calculation from impact×urgency, SLA deadline calculation, overdue detection, SLA breach notification service
acceptance_criteria: Priority is auto-calculated from impact and urgency (e.g. High×Critical = P1)
SLA deadline is computed as created_at + SLA duration for the priority
Overdue incidents are flagged with is_overdue = true
SLA breach triggers a notification event
SLA config can be updated (affects future incidents only)

tasks.description: Implement Incident Management REST API endpoints: CRUD for incidents, status transition endpoint, get by ID with full details, list with filters (status, priority, assignee, date range), assign incident endpoint, search by keyword
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/incidents creates incident with auto-calculated priority and SLA
GET /api/v1/incidents returns paginated list with filter query params
GET /api/v1/incidents/{id} returns full incident with status history
PUT /api/v1/incidents/{id} updates editable fields (not status)
DELETE /api/v1/incidents/{id} soft-deletes
POST /api/v1/incidents/{id}/transition validates and applies status change
PUT /api/v1/incidents/{id}/assign assigns agent, sets status to Assigned

tasks.description: Implement Incident Management UI: incident list view with filters, create/edit form, detail view with status history timeline, status transition buttons, assign dialog, priority badge, SLA timer display
acceptance_criteria: Incident list page renders with data table, pagination, and filter controls
Create incident form includes all required fields with validation
Detail page shows full incident info, status history timeline, and SLA countdown
Status transition buttons are contextual (only valid next states shown)
Assign dialog shows user search and sets agent on submit
SLA timer color-codes: green (on track), yellow (at risk), red (breached)
Priority is displayed as color-coded badge (P1=red, P2=orange, P3=yellow, P4=green)

tasks.description: Implement Service Request Management data models: ServiceRequest model, ServiceCatalogItem model, ApprovalRequest model, FulfillmentTask model, database migrations
acceptance_criteria: ServiceRequest model has fields: id, title, description, requester_id, catalog_item_id, status, priority, approval_status, timestamps
ServiceCatalogItem model has: id, name, description, category, approval_required, estimated_fulfillment_time, is_active
ApprovalRequest links to service_request, has approver_id, status (pending/approved/rejected), comments, decided_at
FulfillmentTask has: id, service_request_id, assigned_to, status, notes, completed_at
All models have proper foreign keys and relationships

tasks.description: Implement Service Request catalog and approval workflow: catalog browse, request submission, multi-level approval chain, approval/rejection logic, auto-notify on status change
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/service-catalog returns active catalog items
POST /api/v1/service-requests creates request with auto-approval or pending
GET /api/v1/service-requests returns user's requests (or all for agents/admins)
POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/approve sets approved, triggers fulfillment
POST /api/v1/approvals/{id}/reject sets rejected with reason
If catalog item has approval_required=false, request is auto-approved
Approval chain supports sequential approvers (optional)

tasks.description: Implement Service Request fulfillment tracking: fulfillment task assignment, progress update, completion marking, fulfillment status dashboard
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/fulfillment requests creates fulfillment task on approval
PUT /api/v1/fulfillment/{id}/progress updates status with notes
PUT /api/v1/fulfillment/{id}/complete marks task done with resolution notes
Fulfillment completion auto-updates service request status to Completed

tasks.description: Implement Service Request Management UI: service catalog browse page, request submission form, user's request list, approval queue for approvers, approval/reject action UI, fulfillment progress tracking
acceptance_criteria: Catalog list shows active items grouped by category
Request form auto-fills catalog item details, allows additional notes
Request list shows status with color-coded badges and timeline
Approval queue (for approvers) shows pending requests with approve/reject buttons
Approval action opens confirmation dialog with optional comment field
Fulfillment progress shows step-by-step status indicator
Request detail page shows full lifecycle from submission to fulfillment

tasks.description: Implement Change Management data models: ChangeRequest model, CAB model, CABMembership model, ChangeSchedule model, database migrations
acceptance_criteria: ChangeRequest model has fields: id, title, description, requester_id, change_type (standard/emergency/normal), priority, status, risk_level, impact_scope, implementation_plan, rollback_plan, cab_id, scheduled_start, scheduled_end, timestamps
CAB model has: id, name, description, is_active
CABMembership links user to CAB with role (chair/member/observer)
ChangeSchedule model: id, change_request_id, scheduled_start, scheduled_end, actual_start, actual_end, status, notes

tasks.description: Implement RFC submission and CAB approval workflow: change request submission, CAB voting/decision, emergency change bypass, risk assessment, approval chain
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/change-requests creates RFC with pending CAB approval
POST /api/v1/cab/{id}/vote records vote (approve/reject/abstain) with comments
CAB decision is finalized when quorum (50%+1) reached or deadline passed
Emergency changes skip CAB approval with justification
Risk level is auto-calculated from impact × probability (optional)
Change request status workflow: Draft→Submitted→CAB Review→Approved/Rejected→Scheduled→In Progress→Completed→Closed

tasks.description: Implement Change schedule calendar: schedule CRUD, conflict detection, calendar view data endpoint, change window enforcement, deployment timeline
acceptance_criteria: PUT /api/v1/change-requests/{id}/schedule sets scheduled_start/end
Conflict detection warns if two changes overlap on same system/scope
GET /api/v1/change-schedule returns calendar events with date range filter
Change window enforcement: certain types restricted to maintenance windows
Approved changes appear on schedule as 'Scheduled' status

tasks.description: Implement Change Management UI: RFC submission form with risk assessment, CAB voting dashboard, change schedule calendar view, change request detail with full workflow, CAB management interface
acceptance_criteria: RFC form includes all required fields with risk assessment section
Change request list shows status, priority, risk with color badges
Detail page shows full lifecycle with CAB votes and schedule
CAB dashboard shows pending votes, meeting schedule, member list
Calendar view renders scheduled changes with color by status/type
Calendar supports month/week/day views
CAB management allows adding/removing members

tasks.description: Implement dashboard and reporting: dashboard widget framework, incident metrics (open counts, SLA breach rate, MTTR), service request metrics, change metrics (success rate, emergency count), data export (CSV)
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows incident summary (open/resolved/total) with charts
SLA breach rate is calculated and displayed
Service request metrics show submitted/approved/fulfilled counts
Change management metrics show success rate and emergency change ratio
Data export downloads CSV of current filtered data
Dashboard auto-refreshes every 60 seconds
